coreFactions.name: The Marquisate
coreFactions.blurb: The Marquise de Cat's industrial occupation, ruling by force from a fortified stronghold.

coreFactions.name: The Eyrie Dynasties
coreFactions.blurb: The old birds of prey, reclaiming their ancestral rule from a network of Roosts.

coreFactions.name: The Woodland Alliance
coreFactions.blurb: The scrappy coalition of denizens fomenting sympathy and open revolt against the powers that be.

coreFactions.name: The Lizard Cult
coreFactions.blurb: Outcast denizens who gather in gardens, spreading their faith among the downtrodden.

coreFactions.name: The Riverfolk Company
coreFactions.blurb: Otter merchants who ply the Woodland's rivers and lakes, trading in coin, mercenaries, and services.

coreFactions.name: The Grand Duchy
coreFactions.blurb: Moles surfacing from below, tunneling up to claim the Woodland with citadels and markets.

coreFactions.name: The Corvid Conspiracy
coreFactions.blurb: Crows for hire whose scattered agents hatch plots in clearings across the Woodland.

coreFactions.name: The Hundreds
coreFactions.blurb: A mass uprising of angry denizens under the Lord of the Hundreds — spreading mobs, seizing hoards, and toppling the powers that be.

coreFactions.name: The Keepers in Iron
coreFactions.blurb: Insular delvers who raise waystations beside the ruins, sending cadres below to recover the relics of the Ancients.

descriptions.makingTheWoodland: The first step to making your Woodland is deciding which factions you are playing with. You must always play with the Denizens, so the general inhabitants of the Woodland are always present. Then select two or three other factions. This companion supports the three core-book factions: the Marquisate, the Eyrie Dynasties, and the Woodland Alliance. Whatever set you choose will shape your Woodland and its implied history.
descriptions.mapOfTheWoodland: Your map always has 12 total clearings. Take a blank sheet of paper. Make one clearing—a single circle near a corner—and roll for its dominant community, number of paths, and name. Draw lines out of the clearing equal to its paths, then draw a new circle at the end of a path and roll again (the existing path counts toward the new clearing's total, so draw only the remainder). Keep the Woodland connected, pointing new paths toward existing ones. You can have at most four clearings of each dominant community, and each name is used only once. Stop at 12 clearings; leave any unfinished paths dangling as failed roads.
descriptions.dominantCommunity: The majority population of denizens in the clearing—foxes, mice, or rabbits. Every clearing holds all denizens, but one community dominates. Birds are never dominant; they spread evenly across the whole Woodland.
descriptions.controlOfClearings: When a campaign begins the war is already underway and the factions have made their opening moves. For each faction you selected, take them in the order below to determine where they begin with presence and power. Treat these rolls as the recent history of the Woodland—note any clearing that changes hands between non-denizen factions; those clearings are "contested" and have seen the most war.
descriptions.woodlandCorner: Choose one corner of the Woodland—a place on the farthest edge of the map. To determine it randomly, roll 1d6 and reroll on a 5–6.
descriptions.marquisate: The chosen corner is the Marquise de Cat's stronghold, from which she manages her occupation. The Marquisate controls that clearing. Then, starting with clearings directly connected to the stronghold, roll on the Marquisate Control table for each clearing by how many paths away it sits. Marquisate control means de facto (if not de jure) command of the clearing.
descriptions.eyrie: The chosen corner (opposite the Marquisate's stronghold, if in play) is the Eyrie's initial Roost. The Eyrie controls that clearing. Then, for each clearing, roll on the Eyrie Control table by how many paths it sits from any Roost. The Eyrie may have at most four Roosts and control at most six clearings (including the initial Roost)—stop once six are controlled. Do not roll for the Marquisate stronghold. Taking a Marquisate-controlled clearing means the Eyrie seized it during the war. Eyrie control is usually de jure—they reinstate and absorb local governments.
descriptions.alliance: Roll on the Woodland Alliance Sympathy table for each clearing, using its current state (Uncontrolled, Controlled, or Contested). Then, for each clearing with sympathy, roll on the Uprising table—continue until you have rolled for every sympathetic clearing or an Uprising takes place (a 10+). Sympathy means the Alliance has functional underground cells and a real undercurrent of support, but is not yet operating openly.
descriptions.uprising: An uprising means the Alliance successfully contested and seized full control of the clearing, overthrowing whoever held it (or partnering with the local denizens where none did). A base means the Alliance now has an open, active headquarters there.
descriptions.denizens: As the last step, roll to see if control of any clearing has returned to the denizens. Do not roll for the Marquisate stronghold, any Eyrie Roost clearing, the Woodland Alliance base clearing, or any uncontrolled clearing. Returning to denizen control usually reflects a faction's withdrawal, retreat, or its local forces going independent—not an Alliance revolt.
descriptions.fleshingOut: For each clearing, generate two of each—inhabitants, buildings, and problems—to determine the fiction of the clearing when you need it.
descriptions.warStatus: To determine how the war has marked a clearing, roll 2d6 + the number of factions controlling or neighboring it (one faction in control of area and neighbors = +1; a different faction on the neighbors = +2; two or more different faction neighbors plus a third in control = +3).
descriptions.expansionOrder: With expansion factions, set up in this order: Marquisate, Eyrie Dynasties, Woodland Alliance, Lizard Cult, Riverfolk Company, Grand Duchy, Corvid Conspiracy, then Denizens. Skip any faction not in play.
descriptions.controlVsPresence: The Lizard Cult, Riverfolk Company, and Corvid Conspiracy build "presence" — a foothold of agents or supporters in a clearing (a bit like sympathy) — before they can act. Presence is binary and does not, by itself, grant control; each of these factions has its own way to convert presence into control. The more militant factions don't track presence.
descriptions.lizard: Roll the Outcast Table for the most downtrodden denizen community — the one most open to the Cult. Give the Lizard Cult presence in two clearings (chosen at random) where that community dominates. Then place a garden and give the Lizard Cult control in a corner of the Woodland — the corner opposite the Marquisate stronghold or an Eyrie Roost if possible; if both are in play, either of the other two corners; if neither, a random corner.
descriptions.riverfolk: The Riverfolk enter by water. Mark which clearings sit on a river or lake, then rank every clearing by how many of these are true: on a river/lake; 3+ paths; 4+ paths; a valuable resource in or near it. The Riverfolk gain presence in the four clearings with the most "yeses." In the single clearing with the most, they gain control and a trading post (break ties randomly; skip a clearing that can't be theirs, e.g. the Marquisate stronghold or an Eyrie Roost).
descriptions.duchy: The Grand Duchy surfaces with a tunnel and an invading force (granting control) in a corner — see the Duchy Invasion Table for which corner, based on the factions in play. Then, for each clearing directly connected to that starting clearing, roll 2d6; on a 10+ the Duchy takes control. Finally, add a tunnel to one random clearing the Duchy does not control.
descriptions.corvid: The Corvid Conspiracy begins with agents scattered wide. Give four random clearings Corvid Conspiracy presence — none of them starts with Corvid control.
descriptions.revisedOrder: With the Ruins & Expeditions factions, the full setup order is: Marquisate, Eyrie Dynasties, Woodland Alliance, Lizard Cult, Riverfolk Company, Grand Duchy, Corvid Conspiracy, the Hundreds, the Keepers in Iron, then Denizens. Skip any faction not in play.
descriptions.ruins: Every Woodland has at least two known ruins, plus more found randomly. After the map is drawn but before assigning faction control, roll 1d6 and halve it (round up) for how many ADDITIONAL ruins dot the Woodland (so 3–5 in total). Each ruin sits at or near a random clearing. Ruins matter for the Keepers in Iron (who build waystations beside them) and for delving expeditions.
descriptions.hundreds: The Hundreds begin in a corner clearing (opposite other corner-focused factions where possible): their stronghold, control, presence, a mob, warriors, and a hoard of Value 30. Then, for each other clearing, roll on the Mob Table (2d6 minus the paths between it and the stronghold) to see if a mob of angry dissenters takes hold. For each mob clearing, roll on the Hoard Table to see what the mob has seized. The Hundreds' stronghold and its control survive the Denizen step.
descriptions.keepers: The Keepers in Iron begin with a waystation beside a ruin (add ruins first if the map has none). Place a waystation in a clearing at or adjacent to a ruin and roll the Keepers' Control table for it; then roll the Second Waystation table for a second waystation beside the next-nearest ruin. The Keepers usually hold presence, not control — they want relics, not clearings. Their waystations survive the Denizen step (control may not).

clearingName.note: Roll two d6: one die selects the row (1–6), the other selects the column band (1–2, 3–4, 5–6). On a duplicate, read the dice the other way or reroll.

byPaths.text: 5+, Marquisate in control. 4–, not in control.
7+, Marquisate in control. 6–, not in control.
10+, Marquisate in control. 9–, not in control.
12, Marquisate in control. 11–, not in control.
5– not in control. 6–8 in control, no Roost. 9+ in control with a Roost.
8– not in control. 9–10 in control, no Roost. 11+ in control with a Roost.
10– not in control. 11 in control, no Roost. 12 in control with a Roost.

ranges.result: Uprising — existing control removed; Woodland Alliance base placed in the clearing
Uprising — control removed; base placed; sympathy placed in every connected clearing

duchyInvasion.note: Which corner the Duchy invades, by the factions in play.

rows.when: Marquisate, Eyrie, and Lizard Cult all in play
rows.corner: The only corner no other faction used as its starting point

rows.when: Any two of those factions in play
rows.corner: Either of the two remaining corners, at random

rows.when: Any one of those factions in play
None of those factions in play

mob.note: Roll 2d6 minus the paths between the clearing and the Hundreds stronghold.

ranges.result: A mob forms only if another faction controls the clearing
Destroy up to two enemy structures; hoard Value 5 per structure destroyed (none if no structures)
Destroy all enemy structures; hoard Value 10 + 5 per structure destroyed
In control only if the clearing is uncontrolled
A second waystation only if they control the first waystation's clearing

factionRoll.description: As you play, the war rages on with or without the vagabonds. When time passes — the spotlight jumps forward a couple of weeks (a long journey, a rest, downtime) — roll for each non-denizen faction to represent the war's ebb and flow.
factionRoll.order: Roll starting with the faction that controls the most clearings, then work down the list.

modifiers.label: Operated unfettered by PC actions since the last roll
Recently suffered a blow from the PC vagabonds
Controls the most clearings (a juicy target)
Holds a valuable resource (from a prior Obtain)

conditions.The Marquisate: 5+ clearings, or any workshop, sawmill, or recruiter
conditions.The Woodland Alliance: 3+ sympathy, or at least one base
conditions.The Grand Duchy: 2+ clearings and at least one market and one citadel

results.10+: Great victory — choose one minor boon, then another minor boon, or a major boon instead.
results.miss: Defeat — the faction loses one: control of a clearing (back to its denizens); a fortification or structure (Roost, base, Marquisate building, garden, citadel, market, trading post, or other); or a valuable resource.

minorBoons.name: Attack
minorBoons.description: Take control of a clearing adjacent to one already controlled. If it has fortifications, destroy those instead. If it has no fortifications but has a Roost, a Woodland Alliance base, any Marquisate structures, or a Grand Duchy citadel or market, destroy all of those instead.

minorBoons.name: Fortify
minorBoons.description: Fortify a controlled clearing. The next time an enemy would take control of a fortified clearing, they only remove the fortifications instead.

minorBoons.name: Obtain
minorBoons.description: Gain a valuable resource. On the next faction roll, the faction takes +2 if it retains the resource.

minorBoons.name: Establish cells
minorBoons.description: Add sympathy to any two clearings.

minorBoons.name: Stamp cells
minorBoons.description: Remove sympathy from all clearings the faction controls.

minorBoons.name: Build industry
minorBoons.description: Construct one sawmill, workshop, or recruiting post in each of two different controlled clearings.

minorBoons.name: Begin/finish building garden
minorBoons.description: Begin a garden in a clearing with Lizard Cult presence, or finish one begun on a prior roll.

minorBoons.name: Proselytize
minorBoons.description: Add Lizard Cult presence to any clearing.

minorBoons.name: Conduct commerce
minorBoons.description: Add Riverfolk Company presence to any clearing.

minorBoons.name: Build trading post
minorBoons.description: Construct a trading post in a clearing with Riverfolk Company presence.

minorBoons.name: Connect tunnel
minorBoons.description: Build a tunnel to any clearing, making it “adjacent” to the Burrow for the Duchy.

minorBoons.name: Build market or citadel
minorBoons.description: Build a market or a citadel in a controlled clearing.

minorBoons.name: Expand network
minorBoons.description: Add Corvid Conspiracy presence to up to two clearings adjacent to its presence.

minorBoons.name: Enact plot
minorBoons.description: Launch a plot in a clearing with Corvid Conspiracy presence.

minorBoons.name: Stamp plot
minorBoons.description: Remove all plots from all clearings the faction controls.

minorBoons.name: Incite mob
minorBoons.description: Add a Hundreds mob to any clearing adjacent to a clearing with a mob or the Hundreds stronghold.

minorBoons.name: Build hoard
minorBoons.description: Roll 1d6, halve (round up); destroy that many enemy structures (non-fortifications first) and build a hoard worth 5x that (or a Value-5 hoard if there are none). That clearing can't build a hoard again until it recovers.

minorBoons.name: Send cadre
minorBoons.description: Add Keepers in Iron presence to any clearing.

minorBoons.name: Move waystation
minorBoons.description: Move a Keepers waystation to another clearing with Keepers presence.

majorBoons.name: Revolt
majorBoons.description: A revolt occurs in a clearing with sympathy; the Woodland Alliance gains a base there, taking control and removing all other faction structures.

majorBoons.name: Build Roost
majorBoons.description: Add a Roost to a clearing the Eyrie controls without a Roost.

majorBoons.name: Capture
majorBoons.description: Seize an important figure in an enemy faction; that faction takes -1 on all faction rolls while that figure is held captive.

majorBoons.name: Rapidly build garden
majorBoons.description: Begin and finish a garden in a clearing with Lizard Cult presence, and take control of that clearing.

majorBoons.name: Trade war
majorBoons.description: Take control of a clearing with a trading post, and add a trading post to an adjacent clearing.

majorBoons.name: Culminate plot
majorBoons.description: Bring a plot to fruition, giving the Conspiracy control of the clearing (the plot remains active).

majorBoons.name: Wild uprising
majorBoons.description: A full uprising in a clearing with a mob and a hoard: the Hundreds take control, add warriors, and remove all other faction structures. The hoard is sent back to the stronghold.

majorBoons.name: Establish waystation
majorBoons.description: Add a Keepers waystation to a clearing with Keepers presence that has no waystation yet.

majorBoons.name: Discover ruins
majorBoons.description: In a Keepers waystation clearing with the fewest proximate ruins, discover a new ruin — add a ruin near that clearing.
